briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: The flagship. Understand the whole product, then surface prioritized opportunities across UI, UX, retention, engagement, community, and beauty.
briefs.title: Product Improvement Discovery

briefs.question: what could this be?
briefs.tagline: How user signal re-enters the product — corrections captured or evaporating, thumbs nobody reads, and the complaint-to-fix path.
briefs.title: Feedback Loop Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Trace latent bugs, unhandled edge cases, and race conditions; rank by user pain with repro paths and fix sketches.
briefs.title: Bug Hunt

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Map what is tested against what is riskiest, and produce a test-writing plan that buys the most safety per hour.
briefs.title: Test Gap Audit

briefs.question: does it work?
briefs.tagline: Find swallowed errors, missing logs, and monitoring blind spots — everywhere the system can break without anyone knowing.
briefs.title: Silent Failure Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Bundle weight, slow queries, N+1s, wasted renders, and missing caches — ranked by latency the user actually feels.
briefs.title: Performance Audit

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Simulate 10x users, data, and traffic on paper — find what breaks first, at what threshold, and the cheapest mitigation.
briefs.title: 10x Stress Review

briefs.question: does it scale?
briefs.tagline: Decompose the wall-clock of a real user action into a per-stage budget — find the tail, the serial chains, and the wait nobody measured.
briefs.title: Latency Budget Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: A defensive review of your own codebase — auth gaps, injection surfaces, exposed secrets, and data leaks, ranked by exploitability.
briefs.title: Security & Privacy Audit

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Vulnerable, abandoned, oversized, or duplicated packages — with a safe upgrade order and removal candidates.
briefs.title: Dependency Health Check

briefs.question: is it safe?
briefs.tagline: Keyboard navigation, contrast, labels, and screen-reader flow — audited end-to-end along the core user journey.
briefs.title: Accessibility Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Reconstruct the signup → activation → habit funnel from code, count the steps, and rank every drop-off point.
briefs.title: Funnel Friction Audit

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Research real rivals on the web, then split the gaps: table stakes you must match vs differentiation bets only you can make.
briefs.title: Competitive Gap Scan

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Find the premium-worthy features, the natural upgrade moments, and the billing friction between users and paying you.
briefs.title: Monetization Map

briefs.question: does it grow?
briefs.tagline: Where an LLM feature genuinely earns its place — automation, drafting, summarization — and where it would be a gimmick.
briefs.title: AI Opportunity Scan

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Duplication, tangles, and dead patterns — mapped as interest paid vs principal owed, with a refactor sequence that pays for itself.
briefs.title: Tech Debt Map

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Walk clone → install → run → change → PR like a brand-new developer and log every stumble on the way to a first contribution.
briefs.title: New-Dev Onboarding Audit

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Census the drifted colors, spacing, and duplicate components; propose the token set and migration order to unify them.
briefs.title: Design System Consolidation

briefs.question: can others build on it?
briefs.tagline: Is your repo legible to a coding agent — CLAUDE.md, discoverable conventions, one-command checks, and tools an agent can actually run.
briefs.title: Agent Readiness Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Follow the docs literally to find where they lie, then rank the gaps by who gets blocked — and which wrong docs to delete.
briefs.title: Documentation Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Extract every user-facing string; hunt jargon, vague CTAs, and unhelpful errors; rewrite the worst ten before/after.
briefs.title: Copy & Voice Audit

briefs.question: is it understood?
briefs.tagline: Naming, error shapes, status codes, and versioning — find the inconsistencies and sequence fixes from non-breaking to breaking.
briefs.title: API Contract Review

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Missing indexes, absent constraints, JSON blobs hiding structure — with a migration plan sequenced safest-first.
briefs.title: Schema Review

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Which user behaviors are invisible today — and the tracking plan that makes the five questions that matter answerable.
briefs.title: Analytics Coverage Audit

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Trace where personal data enters, spreads, and lingers — then run the delete-request test: could you honor one today?
briefs.title: PII & Retention Map

briefs.question: is it sound?
briefs.tagline: Mine the git history for churn, bug magnets, coupled files, and bus-factor silos — predict where the next bug lands.
briefs.title: Git Hotspot Mining

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Map merged-PR to production; every manual step is a finding. Includes the rollback-readiness verdict.
briefs.title: Release Pipeline Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Expensive queries, chatty API loops, unbounded storage, oversized resources — savings ranked by effort.
briefs.title: Cost Audit

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: The 3am table — for each failure scenario: how you'd detect it, how you'd respond, and the gap in between.
briefs.title: Incident Readiness Review

briefs.question: does it run?
briefs.tagline: Env-var sprawl, prod/dev drift, magic values, and settings with no safe default — mapped so the config stops being a source of outages.
briefs.title: Config & Environment Audit

briefs.question: what should go?
briefs.tagline: Dead code, unreachable routes, stale flags, unused deps — a deletion list grouped by confidence. Git makes it reversible; bias to delete.
briefs.title: Prune Audit

briefs.question: what should go?
briefs.tagline: Abstractions with one implementation, pass-through layers, config nobody changes — ranked by clarity gained per risk.
briefs.title: Simplification Pass

briefs.question: do the reports compose?
briefs.tagline: The capstone. Reads every audit report in the repo, dedupes overlapping findings, and merges them into one sequenced plan.
briefs.title: Roadmap Synthesis

briefs.question: do the reports compose?
briefs.tagline: A fast weekly vitals pass — tests, build, deps, TODOs — diffed against the last run so drift shows up as trend arrows.
briefs.title: Recurring Health Check

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Every system prompt in the repo: contradictions, dead instructions, bloat, and injection surface — with a consolidation plan.
briefs.title: Prompt & Instruction Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Your tools are an API whose consumer is a model — audit names, descriptions, error contracts, and guards on destructive actions.
briefs.title: Tool Design Review

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: How runs end — iteration caps, stop conditions, stuck-state detection, and the true cost of one pathological run.
briefs.title: Loop & Termination Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Reconstruct what actually enters the model's window each step — stale payloads, token hogs, retrieval junk, and buried instructions.
briefs.title: Context Engineering Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: The test-gap audit for nondeterminism — golden sets, regression evals, judge quality, and the drift between eval and production.
briefs.title: Eval Coverage Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: A defensive review of your agent's blast radius — injection paths, unvalidated outputs, over-broad permissions, ungated irreversible actions.
briefs.title: Guardrails Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Model-to-task fit across every call site — where cheaper and faster would do, where quality is starved, and what happens when a provider blinks.
briefs.title: Model Strategy Review

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Can you see why a run did what it did — per-step traces, failure taxonomy, and replaying yesterday's bad run today.
briefs.title: Trace & Replay Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: Cost per completed task, not per call — retry waste, context bloat priced out, cache leverage, and the margin math.
briefs.title: Token Economics Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: What the agent carries between runs — persistence scope, staleness, contradictory facts, and memory injected as truth without provenance.
briefs.title: Memory & State Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: The RAG pipeline end to end — chunking, embedding fit, relevance floors, and whether retrieval quality is measured or merely hoped.
briefs.title: Retrieval Quality Audit

briefs.question: does the agent deliver?
briefs.tagline: For systems with more than one agent — role overlap, orchestration fit, communication overhead, and whether the split earns its complexity.
briefs.title: Multi-Agent Topology Review

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: Crash the automation at step 3 of 7 on paper — resumability, idempotency, duplicate side effects, poison inputs, dead letters.
briefs.title: Workflow Resilience Audit

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: LLM calls that should be plain code, rigid code that should be judgment — redraw the line between deterministic and model-driven.
briefs.title: Determinism Boundary Audit

briefs.question: does the process hold?
briefs.tagline: Every agent-to-human and agent-to-agent baton pass — context dropped, work duplicated, escalations landing where nobody looks.
briefs.title: Handoff Audit

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: What waiting on an agent feels like — progress honesty, interruptibility, showing the work, and salvaging partial results.
briefs.title: Agent Experience Audit

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: Approval gates audited for rubber-stamp fatigue and silent damage — who reviews what, when, with enough context to actually judge.
briefs.title: Human-in-the-Loop Placement

briefs.question: does the human trust it?
briefs.tagline: Confidence theater vs honest hedging — uncertainty channels, citation integrity, and what the product does after being wrong.
briefs.title: Trust Calibration Audit

briefs.question: what needs attention?
briefs.tagline: Fifteen-minute recon that reads the repo and tells you which briefs to run, in what order, and why — the router for the whole catalog.
briefs.title: Audit Triage

briefs.question: shall we fix it?
briefs.tagline: The one brief that changes code. Reads any audit report, asks which findings to implement, then fixes them safely — one commit each, re-checked.
briefs.title: The Fixer

playbooks.desc: First contact with an unfamiliar codebase: what is it, is it broken, is it safe, can I work on it.
playbooks.name: Day-1 New Repo

playbooks.desc: The night-before checks: safe, accessible, deployable, ready for a bad day.
playbooks.name: Pre-Launch

playbooks.desc: Make it smaller and clearer: delete, simplify, then map what debt remains.
playbooks.name: Spring Cleaning

playbooks.desc: Ten minutes, trend arrows. Re-run every week.
playbooks.name: Weekly Vitals

playbooks.desc: First contact with an agent codebase: read its mind, its hands, its loops, its traces.
playbooks.name: Agent Day-1

playbooks.desc: Before an agent touches real users: guarded, evaluated, supervised, affordable.
playbooks.name: Agent Ship-Check

playbooks.desc: The full loop: scout the repo, run what it needs, then implement the findings. Between stage 1 and 3, run whichever briefs Triage recommends.
playbooks.name: Triage & Fix

playbooks.desc: Make RAG answers better: see what enters the window, audit the retrieval pipeline, then lock quality with evals.
playbooks.name: Retrieval Tune-Up
